skills.name: JSX
skills.desc: JSX (JavaScript XML) is a syntax extension for JavaScript used in React to define the structure of UI components in a declarative way.

skills.name: Components and Props
skills.desc: Components are the basic building blocks in React, and props are used to pass data from parent to child components.

skills.name: State and Lifecycle
skills.desc: State allows components to store and manage data that can change over time, and lifecycle methods enable control over component behavior.

skills.name: Handling Events
skills.desc: React allows you to handle user interactions and events, making the application interactive and responsive.

skills.name: Conditional Rendering
skills.desc: Conditional rendering enables showing different content based on certain conditions, improving UI flexibility.

skills.name: Lists and Keys
skills.desc: When rendering lists of elements, using keys is crucial to help React identify and efficiently update elements.

skills.name: Context API
skills.desc: The Context API provides a way to share data between components without having to pass props through intermediate components.

skills.name: React Hooks
skills.desc: Hooks allow functional components to manage state and side effects, offering a more concise way to work with React features.

skills.name: CSS in React
skills.desc: Incorporating CSS in React applications to style components using standard CSS stylesheets.

skills.name: Using useState and useReducer
skills.desc: useState and useReducer are hooks for managing local component state, allowing components to react to changes.

skills.name: Using Context for State
skills.desc: Context in React can be used to share and manage global state across components, reducing prop drilling.

skills.name: React Router Basics
skills.desc: React Router is a library for adding client-side routing to React applications, allowing navigation between different views.
